raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "noticing and reporting a phenomenological distinction between mental phenomena and epistemic standards",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "automatic thoughts constitute a distinct category of mental events that can be observed",
    "evidence about what is true exists independently of automatic cognition",
    "the speaker possesses the capacity for meta-awareness that separates these domains",
    "alignment between thoughts and truth is not automatic but requires discernment"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a report of differentiated awareness; presupposes a dualism between subjective process and objective warrant without contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "a framework in which cultivating such discernment reduces identification with transient mentation and supports alignment with deeper or transcendent truth",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "a context that treats all cognition as illusory or equates noticed separation with ontological proof that thoughts never reflect truth"
}
